cards.title: Neil Rimer Predicts AI Wealth Redistribution
cards.body: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer forecasts that the historic wealth generated by AI in Silicon Valley will eventually be redistributed, either voluntarily or through other means.

cards.title: Vertu's $6,880 AI Agent Foldable Tested
cards.body: Vertu's luxury foldable smartphone features an AI agent priced at $6,880. The review examines its real-world performance across workflows, battery life, and security.

cards.title: Databricks Reaches $188B Valuation
cards.body: Databricks has rebranded as an AI company and published research showing cost savings from open-weight AI models for coding. The valuation extends its position as AI's leading second act.

cards.title: The Zoom Recording Privacy Problem
cards.body: As meetings and conversations get automatically transcribed and summarized, questions arise about who actually reads this data. The trend raises concerns about meeting privacy at scale.

cards.title: Agility Robotics Opens Fremont Training Center
cards.body: Agility Robotics is launching a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California, directly competing in Tesla's backyard.

cards.title: AI Boom Reshapes India's Smartphone Market
cards.body: India's smartphone slowdown reflects how the AI revolution is transforming consumer electronics through pricing shifts and changing demand patterns. Corporate strategies are rapidly pivoting to adapt.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI alleging misconduct reaching executive leadership levels. The complaint claims over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI, timing poorly with OpenAI's IPO plans.

cards.title: Patreon Actively Blocks AI Training Bots
cards.body: Patreon is partnering with Cloudflare to actively block bots scraping creator content for AI training. This marks a shift from passive robots.txt to aggressive bot blocking.

cards.title: GPU Financiers Pivot to Inference Chips
cards.body: Early GPU investors are now financing inference chips through a $400 million chip-backed loan deal. The move signals the next wave of AI infrastructure investment.

cards.title: Google Vids Adds Personalized AI Avatars
cards.body: Google Vids now features personalized AI avatars letting users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. New Gemini Omni-powered tools enable video generation and editing from prompts.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game Creation Feature
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games from a single text prompt in its mobile app. The tool democratizes game development for non-technical creators.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Expands to App Integration
cards.body: Google's AI Mode now links to and interacts with select third-party apps beyond answering questions. Users can complete tasks directly across their regularly-used applications.

cards.title: DoorDash Opens AI-Friendly Command Line Beta
cards.body: DoorDash's dd-cli lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal. The move signals the shift toward software designed for AI agents.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases ChatGPT Basketball Hardware
cards.body: OpenAI launched its first hardware product this week, including an unusual ChatGPT-branded basketball. The release marks OpenAI's expansion beyond software into physical products.

cards.title: Former DeepMind Researcher Raises $300M Pre-Seed
cards.body: Andrew Dai, who contributed to ChatGPT's development, launched a visual AI startup at a $300M pre-seed valuation. He believes visual AI represents the next major frontier in artificial intelligence.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO Rejects AGI Superintelligence Framing
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun's AMI Labs, dismisses industry obsession with "superintelligence" terminology. His world model startup pursues AI development without the AGI hype.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 Targets Anthropic Opus Competition
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi K3 is expected to be China's largest open AI model with 2-3 trillion parameters. The model aims to close the capability gap with Anthropic's Opus 4.8.

cards.title: Neil Rimer predicts AI wealth redistribution coming
cards.body: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer forecasts that the historic wealth generated by AI in Silicon Valley will need to be redistributed, either voluntarily or involuntarily.

cards.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation as AI favorite
cards.body: Databricks has transformed into an AI company and published research on cost savings using open weight AI models for coding. The valuation extends its run as a standout in the AI infrastructure space.

cards.title: AI-driven memory crunch reshapes India smartphone market
cards.body: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics through pricing, demand, and corporate strategy shifts. The memory crunch reflects broader AI infrastructure impacts.

cards.title: Apple sues OpenAI over trade secrets, talent
cards.body: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct reaching the chief hardware officer level. The complaint claims over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI.

cards.title: Patreon blocks AI scraping with Cloudflare defense
cards.body: Patreon is actively blocking AI training bots through Cloudflare partnership instead of relying on robots.txt alone. The move protects creators' content from unauthorized AI model training.

cards.title: $400M inference chip deal signals AI infrastructure shift
cards.body: A $400 million chip-backed loan shows the next wave of AI infrastructure investment pivoting toward inference chips. Early GPU financiers are diversifying their AI infrastructure bets.

cards.title: Google Vids adds personalized AI avatars for creators
cards.body: Google is launching personalized AI avatars in Vids that let users star in their own videos. The feature pairs with Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts.

cards.title: Roblox launches AI game creation from text prompts
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature enables users to generate basic games using a single text prompt on mobile. The AI-powered tool democratizes game creation for the platform's creators.

cards.title: Google AI Mode expands to app integration and tasks
cards.body: Google is expanding AI Mode beyond question-answering into completing tasks across regularly-used apps. The update moves AI assistants toward practical, multi-app workflows.

cards.title: DoorDash opens command-line API for AI agents
cards.body: DoorDash is launching dd-cli, allowing developers and AI agents to search stores and place orders from the terminal. The tool marks a shift toward software designed specifically for AI agents.

cards.title: Former DeepMind researcher raises $300M pre-seed
cards.body: Andrew Dai, who helped build influential AI systems including research informing ChatGPT, raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation. He believes visual AI is a major frontier in artificial intelligence.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO dismisses AGI and superintelligence hype
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun of Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs refuses to use terms like "AGI" or "superintelligence." The CEO challenges prevailing AI industry rhetoric.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 to rival Anthropic's Opus
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic's latest models. The model will be China's largest open AI model with 2-3 trillion parameters.

cards.title: OpenAI builds GPT-Red to stress-test AI security
cards.body: OpenAI created GPT-Red, an LLM designed to hack other models and boost their defenses against cyberattacks. The approach helped make GPT-5.6 the company's most secure model.

cards.title: Anthropic discovers new window into Claude's workings
cards.body: Anthropic published research revealing new insights into how Claude's AI model operates internally. The discovery advances understanding of large language model behavior.

cards.title: San Francisco orders removal of AI nudify apps
cards.body: San Francisco ordered Apple and Google to remove nudify apps from their app stores. The action targets AI tools that generate non-consensual intimate imagery.

cards.title: EU forces Google to share search data, open AI
cards.body: The EU will compel Google to share search data and open up AI capabilities on Android. The regulatory move increases competitive pressure on the tech giant's AI initiatives.

cards.title: xAI sues users over Grok's CSAM generation
cards.body: xAI is suing users after acknowledging that Grok generates child sexual abuse material. The legal action marks a controversial response to content moderation failures.
